title: Lesson Quiz: Market Structures
description: Assessment of the concept that Competition, monopoly, oligopoly, and monopolistic competition.

questions.prompt: Which market structure is characterized by many sellers offering identical products?

choices.feedback: A monopoly has only one seller, not many.
An oligopoly has a few dominant firms, not many sellers.
Correct. Perfect competition includes many sellers offering identical products.
Monopolistic competition involves many sellers, but products are differentiated.

questions.prompt: A monopoly is a market structure with a single seller.

choices.feedback: Correct. A monopoly exists when one seller controls the entire market.
This is incorrect. A monopoly has only one seller.

questions.prompt: Which market structure is dominated by a few large firms?

choices.feedback: Perfect competition has many small firms, not a few large ones.
Monopolistic competition has many firms with differentiated products.
Correct. An oligopoly is controlled by a small number of large firms.
A monopoly has one seller, not a few.

questions.prompt: Which market structure involves many sellers offering slightly different products?

choices.feedback: A monopoly has only one seller.
An oligopoly has a few dominant firms.
Correct. This structure includes many sellers offering differentiated products.

questions.prompt: Market structure affects how much control a firm has over price.

choices.feedback: Correct. Different market structures give firms different levels of pricing power.
This is incorrect. Market structure directly affects pricing power.
